text: Postpartum return-to-running programmes should begin with a walk-run protocol before progressing to continuous running.
Postpartum return-to-running should include a period of relative rest before initiating a graduated training programme.
Postpartum running programmes should incorporate gradual increases in both duration and intensity rather than rapid progression.
Targeted strength training for key muscle groups should be incorporated alongside a postpartum return-to-running programme.
Postpartum running training should be modified based on musculoskeletal or pelvic floor symptoms, sleep quality, mental health status, lactation demands, and energy availability.
Returning to running postpartum carries risks of musculoskeletal pain and pelvic floor dysfunction that require individualised exercise prescription.
